mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.4% on 2019-02-01 reflects recent rate cuts from late 2018 tightening cycle
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following tightening reversal
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS decline periods do not exceed baseline by at least 50 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate below 4.0% signals tight labor market and strong consumer purchasing power, sustaining corporate earnings growth and equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% on 2019-02-01 indicates tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE < 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales signals robust consumer demand and near-term earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 504440M on 2019-01-01 reflects consumer spending patterns
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or correlation between RSAFS growth and 5-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declining month-over-month signals weakening manufacturing activity and potential economic slowdown, causing equity investors to reduce risk exposure and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 109.67 on 2019-02-01 reflects manufacturing conditions
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown or early recession warning
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite INDPRO decline, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment combined with declining unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market tightness and wage pressure, which equity markets interpret as inflationary risk requiring higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 150606k and UNRATE at 3.8% on 2019-02-01 indicate tight labor market
failure_condition: Mean 20-day SPX returns during triggered periods exceed baseline by > 50 basis points (t-test p < 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts declining combined with rising mortgage rates (proxied by FEDFUNDS) signals deteriorating housing demand and reduced construction-related economic activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1230k on 2019-01-01 reflects housing market conditions
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing stress
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or correlation between joint HOUST-FEDFUNDS signal and 60-day returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth acceleration signals strengthening economic fundamentals and corporate earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice equity risk premiums downward in response to improved macro outlook.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18784.632B on 2018-10-01 reflects real economic output
failure_condition: Mean 60-day SPX returns during high-growth periods do not exceed low-growth periods by at least 100 basis points (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rising above 2.5% combined with elevated unemployment below 4.0% creates stagflationary pressure that equity markets penalize through multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 253.113 and UNRATE at 3.8% on 2019-02-01
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure or late-cycle inflation concerns
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite stagflationary signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
